reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes the informal claim. V is a vector space over F (via `Field F` and `Module F V`), S and T are linear operators in L(V), and `S ∘ T = T ∘ S` (function composition via FunLike coercion) is equivalent to ST = TS as linear maps. The conclusion `Submodule.map S (ker (T - c • LinearMap.id)) ≤ ker (T - c • LinearMap.id)` precisely says S(null(T - cI)) ⊆ null(T - cI), i.e., the null space is invariant under S. The free variable c : F corresponds to the universal quantification "for every λ ∈ F". Working over an arbitrary field rather than specifically ℝ/ℂ is a natural reading since the informal statement does not restrict F and the result holds generally.
